title: cuscuta durangana
intro: Cuscuta durangana, also known as Devil's Claw vine or Durango dodder, is a parasitic plant that belongs to the Cuscuta genus and is native to Mexico and parts of the southwestern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cuscuta durangana include relief from pain, inflammation, and anxiety, as well as improved digestion and immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
